title: Cryopreservation of labyrinthulomycetes with DMSO and horse serum
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"aa7ba","text":"Modified from one of the methods tested by Cox et al. 2009","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: For Aurantiochytrium and Schizochytrium, we've grown cultures in 790By+ (or 1/2 790By+) to late-log or early stationary phase. We have not experimented with how growth phases affects cryopreservation success with these strains. We are still testing other strains; this protocol may not work for Oblongichytrium.
To a 2.0 ml cryovial, add 600 ul cell culture 300 ul horse serum 100 ul DMSO mix gently
Place cyrovials in Mr Frosty (filled with isopropanol as instructed) Place Mr Frosty in -80 freezer overnight
Transfer cryovials to a -80 freezer for storage

Thaw at 30C for 3-5 min, until just melted Pour into ~10 ml growth medium (such as 790) Return to standard growth conditions Sometimes cultures growth is evident the next day; sometimes it takes nearly a week.
creator: Jackie Collier
